title: sorbus umbellata subsp. banatica
intro: Sorbus umbellata subsp. banatica, also known as the mountain ash or rowan, is a subspecies of deciduous shrub or small tree native to Romania and parts of Eastern Europe. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its berries, which contain anthocyanins and other flavonoids that have anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Sorbus umbellata subsp. banatica include reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting the immune system. In traditional herbal medicine, the berries are often prepared as a tea, infusion, or decoction, and may also be used in tincture or powder form to harness their medicinal properties.
